what_it_does: Index file for the claude-design directory: prescribes a canonical reading order for four core docs, a doc-ownership table (who owns what / what each does NOT own), a one-source note declaring BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md canonical for the backend contract, a division of docs by session type (cognition vs interface), and pointers to the research/ evidence base and two studio reconcile patches.
observations: Names a 3-step canonical reading order: BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md → AUTHORING-FE-HANDOFF + AUTHORING-UI-BRIEF → APPLICATION-STRUCTURE-PACK.md
Doc-ownership table has explicit 'Owns' and 'Does NOT own' columns for four docs
Declares BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md as the single source of truth for the backend contract; structure pack cross-references rather than restates
Splits work by session type: cognition session reads seam pack + authoring docs; interface session reads structure pack

Points at research/ as the bounded-read corpus, listing deep/ distillations (fe-structure, surface-intent, seams, contracts-governance, design-substrate, selfdesc-ops, angles-delta) and inventory/ reads inv-A through inv-E
Lists two studio patch files (studio-fe-wiring.patch, studio-suite-mockup-focus.patch) as studio-reconcile material applied during the interface session
References a board address (board://item-c0a2d591 is in the sibling doc, not here) — this README references research/README.md for where the seam pack supersedes deep-reads
standouts: Explicit 'Does NOT own' column per doc — encodes anti-duplication boundaries as data
One-source rule stated as a tie-breaker: if the same content appears in two docs, the seam pack wins
Reading order is keyed to session role (cognition vs interface), not just sequence
conventions: Follows a documentation-as-navigable-corpus pattern: an index note that routes readers rather than restating content
Uses a doc-ownership table to assign single-ownership of each contract area — mirrors the repo's one-source enforcement principle
notes: If briefing someone: this is the table-of-contents and ownership map for the claude-design design corpus — read it first to know which of the four docs answers which question and that the seam pack is authoritative for the backend.
questions: The deep/ list names seven files but the directory may contain fewer/more — does this README's listing stay in sync with the actual directory?
Are the two studio patches still pending application, or already applied? (the README describes them as material 'applied during the interface session' without a status)
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states itself as 'reading order + doc ownership' and delivers exactly a reading order, an ownership table, a one-source note, and pointers to evidence.
apparent_themes: documentation as a navigable, ownership-partitioned corpus
intention_signals: Trying to prevent the claude-design doc set from drifting into duplicated, contradictory contract descriptions by assigning each doc a clear ownership lane and naming one canonical backend source, so a fresh agent knows exactly where to look and which doc wins on overlap.
novelty: The explicit per-doc 'Does NOT own' column and the session-type division of reading paths are an unusual, disciplined way to structure a docs index.
contribution: Contributes the routing/ownership map for the claude-design design corpus — the index that assigns single-ownership of each contract area and the canonical-source tie-breaker, which a fused system would keep as the navigational spine over the four design docs.
summary_for_embedding: An index README for the claude-design directory defining a canonical reading order, a doc-ownership table with explicit non-ownership boundaries, a one-source rule naming BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md authoritative, session-type reading divisions, and pointers to the research evidence base and studio patches.
intention_for_embedding: To route a fresh agent through the claude-design design docs without duplication or ambiguity by assigning each doc an ownership lane and naming the canonical backend contract source.
